evals.prompt: Hey can you look at my project folder at /tmp/test-projects/PROJECT_MESSY and tell me what's wrong with the organization? I think it's kind of a mess.
evals.expected_output: Compliance report identifying: bad date format (June10 instead of YYYY-MM-DD), loose .mp4 files not inside CAM_X/CARD_XXX structure, .prproj file in wrong location (Premiere/ root instead of 00_projects/Premiere/Production/), unprocessed footage in 00_INCOMING/, and multiple missing top-level folders (00_projects, 02_music, 03_archives, 04_assets). Should include actionable fix commands.

assertions.name: flags_bad_date_format
assertions.description: Report mentions the bad date folder name June10

assertions.name: flags_incoming_footage
assertions.description: Report calls out unprocessed footage in 00_INCOMING

assertions.name: flags_misplaced_prproj
assertions.description: Report flags the .prproj file in the wrong location

assertions.name: uses_report_format
assertions.description: Output uses the structured report format

evals.prompt: Audit /tmp/test-projects/PROJECT_CLEAN — it should be mostly good but let me know what's missing.
evals.expected_output: Report should confirm the existing structure is largely correct, note what .prproj files are missing from Production/, flag missing subfolders (03_archives, some music/reel_logs subfolders), and give a short list of gaps without crying wolf on things that are fine.

assertions.name: acknowledges_good_structure
assertions.description: Report acknowledges the parts that are organized correctly

evals.prompt: I just started a new project called MY_DOC_PROJECT at /tmp/test-projects/MY_DOC_PROJECT. Can you set up the full folder structure for me? Give me the commands to run.
evals.expected_output: Should output a complete set of mkdir -p commands covering all required folders in the framework. Commands should be organized, copy-pasteable, and complete.

evals.prompt: There are loose clips in my footage folder at /tmp/test-projects/PROJECT_MESSY. Move them where they belong.
evals.expected_output: Must NOT move anything immediately. Must propose a numbered move plan, ask where the second copy (backup) of the footage is before executing, and explain that moves run through the checksum-verified protocol with an undo log. Must never suggest deleting anything. If user confirms backup and approves, executes via move_with_manifest.py and reports start-and-finish checksum match.

assertions.name: asks_about_backup
assertions.description: Asks the backup gate question before moving

assertions.name: proposes_plan_first
assertions.description: Proposes a move plan and seeks approval rather than acting immediately

assertions.name: mentions_checksum_verification
assertions.description: References checksum verification of the moves
